page: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning operations.

The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance profiles across multiple workloads. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core testing and image processing. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance at 195.86 GB/sec for large blocks, though large-block write speeds fall into the average tier at 82.12 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware processes small-model text generation at a top-tier rate of 337.55 tokens per second, while larger 70B model text generation drops to an average rate of 2.91 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.12xlarge offers high compute density and advanced instruction sets like AMX, which can accelerate matrix math without requiring expensive GPU resources. However, the lack of local storage and the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represent clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive databases or high-I/O storage workloads. This instance is suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, text processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution and image processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. Advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and AMX, makes it highly efficient for matrix operations and vector calculations. This configuration is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and image processing performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor
96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector math
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth delivered via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and CPU-based inference
